title: Investigate feed-forward vs per-scene reconstruction open question
stumbles: lint_report tool call returned exists=false because the demo corpus has not been linted yet; the agent skipped that source of evidence and continued with what `ask` had already returned.

First pass silently dropped Instant3D from the feed-forward family on a wrong assumption it was a text-to-3D paper out of scope; the researcher caught it and the agent re-fetched the body, classified Instant3D's diffusion stage as feed-forward (sampling a generative prior, not optimising a per-scene loss), and re-ranked the family.
summary: Investigated the inference-cost-vs-fidelity open question. Read the existing question doc, enumerated the LRM-family and SDS-on-splats families, and used `ask` for a synthesis pass. The researcher then pointed out that Instant3D had been dropped without reading, so the agent re-fetched it and re-ranked the feed-forward family with a discussion of whether the diffusion sampler counts as per-scene optimisation. The researcher then demanded a single-architecture ablation rather than a cross-paper benchmark; agent designed a concrete LGM-as-warm-start ablation with three arms (frozen feed-forward, warm-start + N-step fine-tune sweep, per-scene from scratch) on a fixed 25-scene set.
